title: Complete .expect() to Result<T, GameError> migration
description:
Widespread .expect() usage can crash API server. Only 15% migrated.

Locations:
- game/src/games.rs: 21 instances
- game/src/tributes/mod.rs: 2 instances
- api/src/games.rs: 8 .unwrap() calls

Critical Examples:
```rust
// game/src/games.rs:88 - Can crash simulation
clear_messages().expect("Failed to clear messages");

// game/src/games.rs:226 - EMPTY ERROR MESSAGE
.expect("");

// api/src/games.rs:491 - Can crash API server
let game: Option<Game> = response.take(0).unwrap();
```

GameError enum already exists (game/src/games.rs) but needs expansion.

Tasks:
1. Expand GameError enum with all error cases
2. Convert all functions to return Result<T, GameError>
3. Replace .expect() with proper error propagation
4. Update API layer to convert GameError to HTTP errors
5. Fix empty error messages
6. Replace unsafe HashMap .unwrap() with proper handling

Acceptance Criteria:
- Zero .expect() calls in hot paths
- All functions return Result<T, GameError>
- Descriptive error messages
- Tests verify error handling
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor GLOBAL_MESSAGES to per-game queues
description:
Global mutable state prevents parallel game execution and violates pure functional core.

Location: game/src/messages.rs:32-33

Problem:
```rust
pub static GLOBAL_MESSAGES: Lazy<Mutex<VecDeque<GameMessage>>> = ...
```

Impact:
- Prevents parallel game execution
- Violates pure functional core promise
- WebSocket broadcasts now depend on global state
- No message rotation (unbounded queue)

Fix:
```rust
pub struct GameMessages {
    queues: HashMap<String, VecDeque<GameMessage>>,  // Key = game_id
}

// Or return messages from functions:
pub fn run_cycle(&mut self, game: &mut Game) -> Vec<GameMessage> {
    let messages = Vec::new();
    // ... simulation emits events
    messages  // Return instead of storing globally
}
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- Remove GLOBAL_MESSAGES static
- Messages stored per-game or returned from functions
- Update all 6 references in messages.rs
- Update API layer to handle per-game messages
- Add message rotation (10k cap)
- Tests pass with parallel game execution
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix RNG determinism - pass RNG to survival_check
description:
Regression: survival_check() creates new SmallRng internally, breaking deterministic replay.

Location: game/src/areas/events.rs:273-378

Problem:
```rust
pub fn survival_check(...) -> SurvivalResult {
    let mut rng = SmallRng::from_rng(&mut rand::rng());  // ❌ NOT deterministic
}
```

Impact:
- Breaks replay functionality
- Can't reproduce game outcomes with same seed
- Testing becomes less reliable

Fix:
```rust
pub fn survival_check(&self, rng: &mut impl Rng, ...) -> SurvivalResult
```

Update all callers in games.rs to pass RNG instance.

Acceptance Criteria:
- survival_check() accepts &mut impl Rng parameter
- All callers updated
- Test: Two games with same seed produce identical outcomes
close_reason: Closed

title: Shared Surreal connection auth race causes intermittent missing games
description:
api/src/main.rs:299-330 `surreal_jwt` middleware calls `state.db.authenticate(jwt)` on the single shared `Arc<Surreal<Any>>` (lib.rs:23). authenticate() mutates connection-level session state. Concurrent requests (GamePage mount fires DisplayGameQ + GamesListQ + timeline-summary near-simultaneously) interleave: request A's query may run with request B's auth context (or none).

Effect: `fn::get_list_games` and game SELECT permission both reference `$auth.id`. All games default to `private: true` (api/src/games.rs:194), so when $auth resolves to wrong user / empty, owner's own games disappear from /api/games response. Manifests as "doesn't always appear in the game list".

Fix options:
1. Serialize auth+request via Tokio Mutex (simple, serializes throughput)
2. Decode JWT in middleware, inject user_id into request extensions, rewrite queries to take `$user_id` param instead of `$auth.id` (preserves concurrency, big refactor)
3. Per-request connection pool (expensive)

Recommend option 1 short-term for correctness, option 2 long-term.
notes: PR #181 opens with option-1 fix: tokio::sync::Mutex held across authenticate + downstream handler in surreal_jwt middleware.
close_reason: PR #181 merged. Tokio mutex serializes authenticate + handler. User confirmed reduced (not eliminated) — residual empty-list reproductions still possible under heavy click/refresh, tracked separately if needed.

title: Areas map fails to render: Area enum serde mismatch with DB string format
description:
**Bug**: `/games/:id` panel that renders `<Map>` shows "Something went wrong" instead of the hex map.

**Root cause**: `Area`'s `Display` impl writes ring sectors as `"Sector 1"` (with a space). `api/src/games.rs::create_game_area` persists that string directly into `area.area`. The frontend then deserializes `AreaDetails.area: Option<Area>` using serde's derived impl, which expects exact variant identifiers (`"Sector1"` — no space). The deserialize fails, `GameAreasQ::run` returns `Err(GameNotFound)`, and `GameAreaList` renders the generic error.

This regression was introduced when the cardinal `North/East/South/West` variants were renamed to `Sector1..6` in PR #174 (their Display values had no spaces, so `area.to_string()` round-tripped through serde fine).

**Fix**: implement custom `Serialize`/`Deserialize` on `Area` that use `Display` / `FromStr`. `FromStr` already accepts both `"sector 1"` and `"sector1"` (case-insensitive), so existing rows with the spaced form will deserialize. New writes will continue to use the spaced form (matching DB convention). Update the inline tests that assert variant-name JSON shape (none currently) and verify with a roundtrip test.

**Acceptance**:
- `<Map>` renders for an existing game with all 7 hex tiles
- New roundtrip test: `serde_json::from_str::<Area>("\"Sector 1\"")` and `from_str("\"Sector1\"")` both succeed
- All existing tests pass
notes: PR #178: https://github.com/kennethlove/hangrier_games/pull/178
close_reason: Merged in #178

title: Day pages and timeline summary always empty: subject prefix query mismatch
description:
**Bug**: Loading any per-day timeline page (`/games/:id/day/N/:phase`) shows "no events", and the timeline summary on game refresh shows zero event counts even when messages exist in the DB.

**Root cause**: `api/src/games.rs::game_day_logs` and `timeline_summary` both filter messages with `WHERE string::starts_with(subject, $identifier)` where `$identifier` is the bare game UUID. But subjects are stored as `game:{uuid}`, `area:{name}`, or `tribute:{uuid}` (see `game/src/games.rs` log emission sites + `api/src/games.rs::save_game`). The bare UUID never prefixes any of those, so the queries always return zero rows. The same broken pattern is in `count_messages_for_game` (api/src/games.rs:872, 907) and `schemas/logs.surql::fn::get_messages_by_game_id`.

**Fix**: add an explicit `game_id: String` column to the `message` schema (indexed). Populate it in `save_game` from the request context. Migrate all `string::starts_with(subject, $game_id)` queries to `WHERE game_id = $game_id`. Backfill is unnecessary since this PR will only see new messages — but if user wants old messages preserved, do a one-time UPDATE deriving game_id from `source.value` for Game-sourced rows + a join through the game's tributes/areas for Tribute/Area-sourced rows. Recommendation: skip backfill (consistent with prior "no DB backwards compat" stance).

**Affected**:
- `api/src/games.rs::game_day_logs`
- `api/src/games.rs::timeline_summary`  
- `api/src/games.rs::save_game` (count check, retention pruning)
- `schemas/logs.surql::fn::get_messages_by_game_id`
- `shared/src/messages.rs` (add field to GameMessage so it round-trips)
- `game/src/games.rs::push_message` (set game_id from self.identifier)

**Acceptance**:
- Day pages render the events that exist for that day/phase
- Timeline summary shows nonzero event_count for periods with messages
- Lib + integration tests pass; new test verifies the day-log endpoint returns nonempty results after running a cycle
notes: PR #177: https://github.com/kennethlove/hangrier_games/pull/177
close_reason: Merged in #177

title: Restore message persistence in API save_game after GLOBAL_MESSAGES removal
description:
The GLOBAL_MESSAGES queue and get_all_messages() function were removed from game/src/messages.rs as part of architectural refactor (see hangrier_games-33r), but api/src/games.rs save_game() still relies on them.

To unblock compilation, the message persistence + WebSocket broadcast block in save_game() has been temporarily stubbed out (treats messages as empty Vec). This means:
- New game events from run_day_night_cycle are NOT persisted to the message table
- New game events are NOT broadcast over WebSocket
- Game logs API endpoints will return only historical data

Fix requires designing the new event collection mechanism per hangrier_games-33r:
- Option A: run_day_night_cycle returns Vec<GameMessage>
- Option B: Game struct accumulates messages field that save_game drains
- Option C: try_log_action writes directly to a per-game queue passed in

Files affected:
- api/src/games.rs save_game() lines ~784-885
- game/src/games.rs run_day_night_cycle() and try_log_action call sites
close_reason: Already resolved by the event-unification work in issue 33r (PRs #109, #110, #111). Verified: Game struct has pub messages: Vec<GameMessage> field with #[serde(skip_serializing)] (game/src/games.rs:74-75), Game::log()/log_output() push to self.messages (games.rs:188-213), and api/src/games.rs:757 drains via std::mem::take(&mut game.messages), persists to DB, and broadcasts over WebSocket. cargo check passes; no GLOBAL_MESSAGES, get_all_messages, save_global_messages_to_db, or message-persistence todo!() remain.

title: Add message rotation with 10k capacity cap
description:
Unbounded message queue causes OOM risk in long games.

Location: game/src/messages.rs

Current: No capacity limits, messages accumulate indefinitely

Risk: Long games (100+ days) could exhaust memory

Fix:
```rust
const MAX_MESSAGES: usize = 10_000;

pub fn add_message(...) -> Result<(), String> {
    let mut queue = GLOBAL_MESSAGES.lock()?;
    if queue.len() >= MAX_MESSAGES {
        queue.pop_front(); // Drop oldest message
    }
    queue.push_back(message);
    Ok(())
}
```

Additional:
- Log warning when approaching 80% capacity
- Consider time-based rotation (keep last 24 hours)
- Add metrics for message queue size

Acceptance Criteria:
- Queue capped at 10k messages
- Oldest messages dropped when full
- Warning logged at 8k messages
- Test verifies rotation works
- No OOM in 100-day game test
close_reason: Closed

Recommendation: Unify under single event sourcing pattern

3. Return events from functions (functional approach)

title: Thread RNG through function parameters instead of recreating
description:
**Context**: Event and item generation creates new SmallRng on every call, wasting CPU and making testing harder.

**Current Behavior**:
- events.rs:78, 85: Creates new RNG for random() and random_for_terrain()
- events.rs:293: Creates new RNG for every survival check
- items/mod.rs: Creates new RNG for every item generation
- Called 50-100+ times per game cycle

**Issues**:
- SmallRng::from_rng() allocates and seeds on every call
- Costs ~5-10µs per creation
- Non-deterministic testing
- Violates pure functional architecture

**Desired Behavior**:
```rust
// Pass RNG through function signatures
pub fn random_for_terrain(terrain: &BaseTerrain, rng: &mut impl Rng) -> AreaEvent {
    Self::iter().choose(rng).unwrap().clone()
}

pub fn survival_check(
    &self,
    terrain: &BaseTerrain,
    has_affinity: bool,
    has_item_bonus: bool,
    is_desperate: bool,
    current_health: u32,
    rng: &mut impl Rng,  // Add this
) -> SurvivalResult {
    // Use provided RNG
}
```

**Benefits**:
- 5-10% faster event processing
- Deterministic testing with seeded RNGs
- Enables replay functionality
- Aligns with pure functional core

**Locations**:
- game/src/areas/events.rs:78, 85, 293
- game/src/items/mod.rs (item generation)
- game/src/brains.rs (terrain affinity)

**Priority**: High - performance + testability
close_reason: Threaded RNG through event generation functions. Updated AreaEvent::random() and random_for_terrain() to accept &mut impl Rng parameter. Updated all callers and test files. Impact: Eliminates 50-100 SmallRng allocations per game cycle (~5-10µs each). Enables deterministic testing with seeded RNGs.

title: Return references in living_tributes() to eliminate cloning
description:
**Context**: living_tributes() clones all living tributes on every call, causing 20-30% performance overhead.

**Current Behavior** (line 93-98):
```rust
pub fn living_tributes(&self) -> Vec<Tribute> {
    self.tributes
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| t.is_alive())
        .cloned()
        .collect()
}
```

**Issues**:
- Called 4+ times per cycle (lines 218, 230, 382, 717)
- Each tribute is ~500+ bytes
- With 24 tributes, clones 48KB+ per cycle
- Also affects recently_dead_tributes(), open_areas()

**Desired Behavior**:
```rust
pub fn living_tributes(&self) -> Vec<&Tribute> {
    self.tributes.iter().filter(|t| t.is_alive()).collect()
}

// Or for mutable access:
pub fn living_tribute_indices(&self) -> Vec<usize> {
    self.tributes.iter().enumerate()
        .filter(|(_, t)| t.is_alive())
        .map(|(i, _)| i)
        .collect()
}
```

**Impact**: 20-30% faster cycle execution, 60-70% reduction in allocations

**Locations**:
- game/src/games.rs:93-98 (living_tributes)
- game/src/games.rs:102-108 (recently_dead_tributes)
- game/src/games.rs:129-131 (open_areas)
- Line 203: Remove unnecessary self.clone()

**Priority**: High - significant performance gain
close_reason: Eliminated tribute cloning in living_tributes_count(). Added new count method that iterates without cloning. Updated 5 call sites to use count() instead of len(). Optimized winner() to only clone the winning tribute. Benchmark: ~45-53ns per count (previously ~4-5µs with cloning).

title: Fix Cornucopia hardcoded string match to use Area enum
description:

**Current Behavior** (line 485-501):

```rust
let area_details = self
    .areas
    .iter_mut()
    .filter(|ad| ad.area.is_some())
    .find(|ad| ad.area.clone().unwrap().to_string() == "Cornucopia")

```

**Issues**:
- Panics if area renamed or missing
- Uses string comparison instead of enum
- Clones area unnecessarily

**Desired Behavior**:
```rust
if let Some(cornucopia) = self.areas.iter_mut()
    .find(|ad| ad.area == Some(Area::Cornucopia)) 
{
    // Add feast items
} else {
    eprintln!("Warning: Cornucopia not found for Day 3 feast");
}
```

**Benefits**:
- Type-safe area lookup
- No panic risk
- Better error handling

**Location**: game/src/games.rs:485-501

**Priority**: High - critical bug risk
close_reason: Fixed Cornucopia hardcoded string match to use Area::Cornucopia enum instead. Changed from string comparison '.to_string() == "Cornucopia"' to direct enum match 'ad.area == Some(Area::Cornucopia)'.

title: Integrate event survival processing into game loop
description:
**Context**: PR #77 added process_event_for_area() function that handles terrain-aware survival checks for tributes during area events. The function exists but isn't called from the game loop yet.

**Goal**: Wire up event survival processing so that when area events are triggered, tributes must survive or suffer consequences.

**Current Behavior**:
- trigger_cycle_events() creates random events and adds them to areas
- Events are announced but don't affect tributes
- process_event_for_area() exists but is never called

**Desired Behavior**:
- When trigger_cycle_events() creates an event, tributes in that area must make survival checks
- Terrain compatibility affects severity (wildfire in forest = catastrophic, in desert = minor)
- Outcomes: death, instant death, damage, sanity loss, stamina rewards, item rewards
- Narrative messages generated for all outcomes

**Implementation Challenge**:
The current loop in trigger_cycle_events() (lines 448-465) iterates over self.areas mutably:
```rust
for area_details in self.areas.iter_mut() {
    // Generate event
    area_details.events.push(area_event.clone());
}
```

But process_event_for_area() needs &mut self. Solution:
1. Collect (area, event) pairs first
2. Then process each pair with process_event_for_area()

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Events trigger survival checks for tributes in affected areas
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
- [ ] Add integration test verifying end-to-end event processing in game loop
- [ ] Messages generated for both event occurrence and survival outcomes

**Location**: game/src/games.rs:438-485 (trigger_cycle_events function)
notes: Implementation completed in PR #78. Event survival processing now integrated into game loop.

title: Day-3 stuck game (5/2 binary) — verify reproduces against fresh binary
description:
User reported a quickstart game stopped advancing at day 3. Inspection: game `57dc7e7c-...` shows day=3, status=InProgress, 0 dead, 24 alive, 144 messages cleanly distributed (day1: 24day+37night, day2: 16day+17night, day3: 23day+27night). Cycles ran fully through day 3 night.

Critical context: this game ran against the stale May-2 api binary (see prior session notes). After restarting the freshly-built binary at 04:55, no /api/games/.../next requests have hit the server — frontend stopped retrying. Need to:
1. Reproduce by clicking "Play day 4" against fresh binary
2. If still stuck: capture /tmp/api.log for next_step error path, check tracing-layer http response code
3. Suspect day-3 ActionSuggestion (Move to Cornucopia at 0.75) crowding interaction or save_game serialization edge case

Likely the frontend mutation just hadn't been retried; this may not reproduce.

title: Sub-tile pathfinding within areas
description:
Follow-up to hangrier_games-8pq (area-graph A*). Once `rzy` (animated tribute movement) defines sub-tile movement/blocking semantics, implement sub-tile-level A* using the same generic graph trait introduced by 8pq.

**Prerequisites:**
- Sub-tile traversability rules (do sub-tile terrain/closures exist as their own concept, or inherited with noise from parent area?)
- Sub-tile blocking model (per-sub-tile events? collision with other tributes?)

**Scope:**
- `SubTileGraph` impl of the generic `Graph` trait from `game::pathfinding`
- Cross-area sub-tile traversal (sub-tile <-> sub-tile through area boundaries)
- Brains pick a sub-tile goal, not just an area goal

Depends on hangrier_games-8pq landing first (defines the trait) and rzy (defines movement semantics).

title: Brainstorm: Shelter + Hunger/Thirst system
description:
Brainstorm and design the shelter system AND a hunger/thirst system together. These are filed as one issue because they are heavily intertwined: shelter is partly about keeping food/water; foraging happens at shelters; hunger/thirst drives Brain to seek shelter.

Context: Out of scope from `2026-05-02-weather-system-design.md` and `2026-05-02-tribute-emotions-design.md`. Weather spec defines "shelter" minimally as `BaseTerrain::UrbanRuins` OR `TributeStatus::Hidden`. Emotions spec marked the "hungry / low on supplies" trigger as conditional on a supplies system existing — currently no such system.

User intent (from brainstorm conversation):
- Shelter: a "found shelter" roll considering weather, terrain, traits, etc. Don't need to be highly realistic.
- Hunger/thirst: add food to map items and sponsor gifts; tributes can starve / die of thirst.

Open design questions for the brainstorm session:

**Shelter:**
- Is shelter a status, an item, a per-area property, or a constructed thing?
- Does it persist across turns or refresh per phase?
- Capacity-limited (only N tributes can shelter in one area)?
- Do allies share shelter automatically?
- Does it interact with the existing `Hidden` status, replace it, or coexist?
- Does shelter provide hunger/thirst recovery (cooking, water collection)?

**Hunger / Thirst:**
- Two `u8` counters per tribute that tick down each phase (or each day)?
- Tick rate: aggressive enough to matter, loose enough to not dominate?
- Which existing attribute affects how long a tribute lasts (Strength? Constitution if added?)
- Tick rate modulated by weather (cold = faster hunger, hot = faster thirst)?
- Damage curve at zero (instant death? gradual health loss?)
- New death status / message templates: "starved", "died of thirst"
- Brain decision branch: when hungry, weight item-hunting + foraging higher; when thirsty, prioritize moving toward water sources
- Foraging mechanic: terrain-dependent chance per phase to find food/water (Wetlands rich, Desert bare)
- Water sources per terrain (rivers in Wetlands, springs in Mountains, none in Desert)
- New item types: food/water with consumption mechanics
- Sponsor gift pool expands to include food/water items
- Map item generation balance: combat items vs survival items
- UI: two more bars on tribute card

Estimated complexity: ~1.5–2x the work of the emotions spec when implementation-ready. Touches Brain, Items, Areas, UI, DB schema.

Defer until: current three specs (emotions, weather, progressive-display) are implemented and the integration points are concrete.

Related: `2026-05-02-tribute-emotions-design.md`, `2026-05-02-weather-system-design.md`, `2026-04-17-terrain-biome-system-design.md`.

title: Subdivide area-hexes into sub-tile grid
description:
Once the v1 hex map (89z) lands, each of the 7 area-hexes (Cornucopia + 6 sectors) renders as a single hex tile. To support pathfinding (8pq), animated tribute movement (rzy), and per-tribute positioning that doesn't overlap, subdivide each area-hex into a finer hex grid of sub-tiles.

**Scope:**
- Decide sub-resolution (likely 7 sub-hexes per area = 49 total, or 19 per area for a Catan-style grid; pick during brainstorming)
- Sub-tile coords (axial within each area, plus area<->sub-tile bijection)
- Tributes get a sub-tile position; rendering offsets them within their area
- Game logic stays at the area level (sub-tile is presentation/movement only) until 8pq lands

**Out of scope for this bead:**
- Multi-hop pathfinding (8pq) — depends on this
- Animated movement (rzy) — depends on this
- Different map orientations (deferred to a separate bead if/when needed)

**Acceptance:**
- Each area-hex visually divided into N sub-hexes with their own rendering
- Multiple tributes in the same area no longer overlap visually
- Game logic unchanged (area-level state)
- Unit tests for sub-tile coord math

title: Hex-tile arena map (replace static 5-area SVG)
description:
Replace the current hand-coded static SVG `Map` (cornucopia + N/S/E/W lobes) with a hex-tile grid renderer.

**Scope:**
- Hex grid math module (axial/cube coords, neighbor lookup, pixel<->hex conversion). Pure Rust, no Dioxus deps; lives in `web/src/components/map/` or a new `shared` module if game logic also wants it.
- New `Map` component renders an SVG `<polygon>` (or `<path>`) per hex tile with:
  - Numeric labels (axial or sequential index — TBD in design)
  - Per-tile fill driven by terrain/area state (open/closed, threat overlay)
  - Hover/click affordances (selecting a tile surfaces it in the existing area panel flow)
- Default layout: produce a hex layout that maps the existing 5 logical areas (Cornucopia center + 4 cardinal lobes) onto a small hex cluster so the game still plays unchanged.
- Backwards-compat: existing `AreaDetails`-based data still drives state; this is a render-layer change.

**Out of scope (separate beads):**
- Pan/zoom/mini-map/keyboard controls (hangrier_games-69g — unblocked once this lands)
- Larger templates (hangrier_games-2ac)
- Procedural generation (hangrier_games-y8c)
- Animated tribute movement (hangrier_games-rzy)

**Acceptance:**
- Game renders on the hex map with the default 5-area layout
- Each hex shows a numeric label and respects open/closed state
- Click-to-select still routes to the existing area detail UI
- Host-side unit tests for the hex math module
- Render-smoke integration test for the new Map component (following `web/tests/map_test.rs` pattern)

title: Brainstorm: Gamemaker Event System
description:
Design a system for Capitol/gamemaker interventions in the arena, separate from natural causation.

Context: The weather-system design (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-weather-system-design.md`) carved the existing `AreaEvent` enum into a three-layer architecture:
- Weather (persistent atmospheric state) — designed
- NaturalHazard (acute geological/atmospheric disasters) — designed
- **GamemakerEvent (Capitol interventions) — DEFERRED to this issue**

Scope of this brainstorm:
- What kinds of interventions exist? (fireballs, mutts, killer monkeys, force-field shifts, area closures, resource drops, tracker-jacker swarms, ...)
- Are gamemakers an actor with state? (drama budget, escalation pressure as the field thins, "we need to spice this up" cadence rules)
- How are interventions triggered? (random per-day with cadence rules, dramatic-pacing-driven, or player-controllable in some game modes?)
- How do they interact with weather + emotions? (mostly orthogonal, but worth designing the interface)
- Do they bypass shelter / hide rules that NaturalHazards respect?
- What does the announcer/UI present?

Deliverable: spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-gamemaker-event-system-design.md`.

Related: emotions spec (`2026-05-02-tribute-emotions-design.md`), weather spec (`2026-05-02-weather-system-design.md`), event severity (`2026-04-17-event-severity-integration.md`), game event enum (`2026-04-26-game-event-enum.md`).

title: Surface persistence failures in create_game / create_tribute (post-UPSERT verify)
description:
Follow-up to the UPSERT fix. Even with UPSERT, a permission rejection on a record write returns an empty result set (no Err) from the SurrealDB SDK. We should defensively verify each create site materialized the row (e.g. SELECT id FROM $rid in the same query, return InternalServerError if empty) so this category of silent persistence bug stops being reachable.

title: audit remaining db.update/create .content() call sites in api/
description: Other .content() call sites in api/src have the same latent SDK serializer risks fixed in PR 143: api/src/games.rs lines ~101 GameArea, ~160 GameAreaEdge, ~209 game create, ~302 Item, ~318 AreaItemEdge; api/src/tributes.rs ~71/90; api/src/auth.rs ~81 RefreshToken. They haven't tripped errors yet but should be migrated to the serde_json::Value + raw UPDATE query pattern preventively. Pattern reference: see save_game in api/src/games.rs after PR 143.
notes:
PR 144 closed two more sites: `save_area_items`/`save_tribute_items` had a hand-rolled `UPDATE item:⟨{id}⟩ CONTENT { ... }` block that silently dropped the `rarity` field (added recently to Item); and `create_game_area` (line ~160) used `db.insert::<Option<GameAreaEdge>>(rid).relation(...)` which fails because RELATE returns an array. Both replaced with `serde_json::to_value` + raw query / raw RELATE.

Updated remaining-site list (line numbers from main as of aaf13beb):
- api/src/games.rs ~101 GameArea (.create or .insert::<Option<X>>)
- ~209 game create (db.insert::<Option<Game>>(...).content(game.clone()))
- ~302 Item (db.insert::<Option<Item>>(rid).content(new_item))
- ~318 AreaItemEdge (db.insert::<Vec<AreaItemEdge>>("items").relation([..]))
- api/src/tributes.rs ~71/90
- api/src/auth.rs ~81 RefreshToken

Add to risk pattern: hand-rolled `{table}:⟨{uuid}⟩ CONTENT { ... }` queries that name fields explicitly are equally dangerous - they silently drop fields added later. Prefer `serde_json::to_value(record)` + bound `UPDATE $rid CONTENT $body` everywhere.
close_reason: Merged in PR #145

title: log output during cycles is wrong
description:
After PR 143, game cycles persist correctly and games advance through days. Live Events panel shows wrong/incomplete output:

Symptoms (from end-of-night-3 with 24 tributes):
- Only ~10 events rendered for an entire day+night cycle (should be many more)
- Events shown look incomplete: mostly 'X is already in the Cornucopia, stays put' and 'X is too exhausted to move from Y, lacks stamina'
- Contradictory pairs in same step: 'Sandy Spencer is too exhausted to move from North, lacks stamina' immediately followed by 'Sandy Spencer moves from North to Cornucopia' (same for Arjun Lueilwitz)
- No combat / death / item / sponsor messages despite ~24 tributes

Hypotheses:
1. Live Events panel re-renders and clobbers prior events on each WebSocket message instead of appending
2. Frontend buffer cap is too small
3. Backend WebSocket broadcaster only emits a subset of MessagePayload variants
4. WebSocket connection drops mid-cycle and reconnects show only the last batch

The 'too exhausted then moves' contradiction is a separate gameplay-logic bug — likely the stamina check fires the message but the move proceeds anyway, or the messages are emitted in the wrong order.

Investigation should cover: web/src/components/* for the Live Events component, WebSocket message handler, and the backend broadcaster in api/src/games.rs / api/src/ws.rs (or equivalent).
close_reason: Merged in PR #146

title: Registration UI: surface password length validation errors
description:
Server validates password is 8-72 chars (shared/src/lib.rs:187, validator crate) and API returns AppError::ValidationError, but the registration form (web/src/components/accounts.rs, RegisterForm around line 240+) only performs client-side checks for empty username and matching passwords. It does not:

1. Enforce min length (8) client-side before submitting
2. Surface the server-side ValidationError back to the user in password_error_signal / username_error_signal

Result: user submits a too-short password, gets a generic/silent failure, retries with different passwords, and has no idea which (if any) attempt actually succeeded. Eventually hits Conflict ("Username already taken") on a username they never knowingly registered.

Acceptance criteria:
- Client-side: enforce username 3-50 and password 8-72 before submit, mirroring shared::RegistrationUser validators (or call .validate() directly from the shared crate).
- Server errors (ValidationError, Conflict, etc.) returned by register_user must be parsed and displayed in the appropriate error signal, not swallowed.
- Same treatment for the LoginForm where appropriate (though login intentionally returns generic Unauthorized).
- Add a test or component story exercising the too-short-password path.
close_reason: Merged via PR #162

title: fix(game): Brain runtime state lost on Tribute serde roundtrip
description:
Tribute.brain has #[serde(skip)] at game/src/tributes/mod.rs:68. Brain (game/src/brains.rs:97-103) holds psychotic_break, preferred_action, preferred_action_percentage, thresholds — all silently reset to default every time a Tribute is loaded from SurrealDB. Brain already derives Serialize+Deserialize so nothing blocks removing the skip.

## Repro
1. Run game; tribute enters Berserk / has preferred_action set
2. API restart or any DB roundtrip
3. Tribute.brain resets — psychotic_break false, preferred_action None, percentages zeroed
4. Behavior diverges from pre-serde state silently

## Fix (chosen approach: Option A)
- game/src/tributes/mod.rs:68 — remove #[serde(skip)] on brain field, add #[serde(default)] so old rows missing the column hydrate via Brain::default
- Tribute table is SCHEMALESS (schemas/tribute.surql:1) so no DDL migration needed
- Verify Action enum (used in Brain.preferred_action) is serde-clean — check game/src/brains.rs

## Rejected alternatives
- Option B: mirror brain fields onto Tribute struct directly. Sync bugs lurk.
- Option C: split Brain into static config + runtime state, only persist runtime. Doesn't fix the bug, adds churn.

## Tests required (TDD)
- Mid-Berserk roundtrip: serialize tribute with psychotic_break=true + preferred_action=Some(Attack), deserialize, assert fields preserved
- preferred_action roundtrip with non-None Action variant
- Missing-field default: deserialize JSON without 'brain' key, assert Brain::default hydrated

## Out of scope
- alliance_events (transient by spec)
- recently_killed_by (transient, shipped in PR #124)
- Other #[serde(skip)] fields on Tribute

## Effort
S, ~30-60 min. Single-file change + 3 tests.

title: Brain runtime state lost on DB serde (psychotic_break, preferred_action)
description:
Tribute.brain is marked #[serde(skip)] at game/src/tributes/mod.rs:63. This silently resets psychotic_break, preferred_action, and preferred_action_percentage to defaults on every load from SurrealDB.

Effects:
- Psychotic breaks 'heal' across save/load boundaries (a tribute mid-Berserk reverts to normal state)
- Preferred-action overrides set by external systems vanish on next load
- Once 0ug lands, the same skip would also wipe turns_since_last_betrayal if we put it on Brain (we won't — going on Tribute per 0ug spec)

Fix: either remove serde(skip) and make every Brain field serde-friendly + add to schema, OR explicitly persist the runtime fields elsewhere on Tribute.

Discovered while auditing Brain for hangrier_games-0ug (alliance system). Out of 0ug scope to keep that change focused on traits + alliances.

title: Resolve tributes auto-spawn vs manual POST design conflict

This means every freshly-created game is already at capacity, making any POST /api/games/{id}/tributes endpoint immediately broken. Tests in api/tests/tributes_tests.rs assume games start empty and tributes are added via POST. Resolution options: (a) add ?empty=true opt-out flag to POST /api/games, (b) remove auto-spawn from create_game and require manual creation, (c) accept current design and update tests to use auto-spawned tributes. Discovered while completing a94 (test verification post PR #119/#120). Tributes integration tests skipped for now.

title: auth: JwtClaims expects lowercase 'id' but SurrealDB-issued JWTs use uppercase 'ID'
description:
The signup/signin flow in api/src/users.rs returns a JWT issued directly by SurrealDB (DEFINE ACCESS user ... WITH JWT in schemas/users.surql). SurrealDB's standard JWT claims use uppercase fields: ID, NS, DB, AC, iat, exp.

However, JwtClaims at api/src/users.rs:21 declares 'id: String, sub: String' (lowercase id) and extract_user_from_jwt at api/src/users.rs:43 calls surrealdb::sql::thing(&claims.id). This works for refresh tokens (our own generate_access_token at api/src/auth.rs:142 emits lowercase 'id') but fails for signup/signin tokens straight from SurrealDB.

Symptom uncovered by hangrier_games-0hw: integration auth_tests fail with 'Failed to decode JWT: JSON error: missing field id at line 1 column 234'.

Fix options to evaluate:
1. Add #[serde(rename = "ID", alias = "id")] on JwtClaims.id (and similar for sub if applicable)
2. Define a separate SurrealJwtClaims struct and try both decoders
3. Customize the SurrealDB ACCESS definition to emit lowercase claims (less portable)

Was masked previously by NS/DB mismatch making signup fail before issuing a JWT. Discovered after hangrier_games-0hw made NS/DB configurable.

Blocks: hangrier_games-a94 (integration test enablement)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #120: JwtClaims accepts uppercase ID + alias id (SurrealDB Record auth claim case), refresh token datetimes use surrealdb::sql::Datetime to match schema,  bind renamed to  (reserved variable), and duplicate username signup maps to 409 Conflict. All 7 auth_tests pass.

title: Implement critical hits and fumbles in combat
description:
d20 combat has no special outcomes for natural 1 or 20 rolls - adds drama.

Location: game/src/tributes/mod.rs:1309-1377 (attack_contest)

Current: All rolls treated equally

Proposed:
```rust
fn attack_contest(...) -> AttackResult {
    let attack_roll = rng.random_range(1..=20);
    let defense_roll = rng.random_range(1..=20);
    
    match (attack_roll, defense_roll) {
        (1, _) => AttackResult::CriticalFumble, // Drop weapon, self-damage
        (20, _) => AttackResult::CriticalHit,   // Triple damage
        (_, 20) => AttackResult::PerfectBlock,  // Defender auto-wins
        _ => { /* existing logic */ }
    }
}
```

Effects:
- Natural 1 (fumble): Drop weapon, take 1 damage, lose turn
- Natural 20 (crit): Triple damage, ignore armor, stun effect
- Defender natural 20: Perfect block, disarm attacker

Impact: Adds excitement, memorable moments, strategic risk

Acceptance Criteria:
- AttackResult enum extended with new variants
- Critical logic implemented
- Messages generated for special outcomes
- Tests verify all combinations
- Balanced probabilities (5% each)
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement personality-based AI thresholds
description:
All tributes use identical decision thresholds - no personality differentiation.

Current Problem:
```rust
const LOW_HEALTH_LIMIT: u32 = 20;  // Same for all tributes
const MID_SANITY_LIMIT: u32 = 35;  // Same for all tributes
```

Impact: Tributes feel identical in behavior

Proposed:
```rust
pub struct BrainPersonality {
    pub aggression: f32,      // 0.0-1.0
    pub caution: f32,
    pub resourcefulness: f32,
}

impl Brain {
    fn effective_low_health_threshold(&self) -> u32 {
        // Aggressive tributes fight at lower health
        (LOW_HEALTH_LIMIT as f32 * (1.0 - self.personality.aggression * 0.5)) as u32
    }
}
```

Examples:
- Aggressive tribute: fights until 10% health instead of 20%
- Cautious tribute: hides at 30% health instead of 20%
- Resourceful tribute: searches more, fights less

Personality Generation:
- Random at creation
- District-based modifiers (District 1 more aggressive)
- Balanced distribution across tributes

Acceptance Criteria:
- BrainPersonality struct added
- Decision thresholds use personality modifiers
- Personality assigned at tribute creation
- Tests verify behavior differences
- Distinct gameplay feel per tribute

title: Implement terrain-specific event generation
description:
**Context**: Currently AreaEvent::random() generates any event regardless of terrain. Tests show we have terrain-appropriate event probabilities defined, but they're not used in actual game logic.

**Current Behavior**:
- trigger_cycle_events() calls AreaEvent::random() which can generate any event
- Wildfire can occur in desert, blizzard in jungle, etc.
- Terrain compatibility only matters for severity during survival checks

**Desired Behavior**:
- Events should be terrain-appropriate by default
- Forests get wildfires, mountains get avalanches, deserts get sandstorms
- Rare "freak events" could still occur (configurable)

**Implementation**:
Replace AreaEvent::random() with AreaEvent::random_for_terrain(terrain: &TerrainType):
```rust
// In trigger_cycle_events()
let area_event = AreaEvent::random_for_terrain(&area_details.terrain);
```

**Benefits**:
- More realistic/immersive
- Predictable danger zones (tributes can strategize)
- Better game balance (no catastrophic mismatches by chance)

**Test Coverage**:
- Tests already exist for terrain-specific generation (test_forest_generates_terrain_appropriate_events, etc.)
- Need to verify they work with actual game loop

**Dependencies**: Should be done after hangrier_games-ae4 (event integration)

**Location**: game/src/areas/events.rs (add random_for_terrain method), game/src/games.rs:450 (call site)
notes: Implementation completed in PR #79. Game loop now generates terrain-appropriate events.

title: Add event configuration system for tunable parameters
description:
**Context**: Event survival system is hardcoded. Need configurable parameters for different game modes and difficulty levels.

**Goal**: Make event system tunable via configuration.

**Configuration Parameters**:
- **Event Frequency**: Per-terrain event spawn rates (currently DAY_EVENT_FREQUENCY/NIGHT_EVENT_FREQUENCY constants)
- **Severity Multipliers**: Global or per-terrain severity scaling
- **Instant Death Toggle**: Enable/disable instant death outcomes
- **Reward Probabilities**: Chance for stamina/sanity recovery, item drops
- **Event Duration**: How long events persist (currently indefinite)

**Implementation Options**:
1. Game-level config in Game struct
2. Global constants in events.rs with environment overrides
3. Per-game settings in database

**Benefits**:
- Easy mode: Lower severity, no instant death
- Hard mode: Higher severity, more frequent events
- Custom games: Player-configurable difficulty
- Balancing: Tune without code changes

**Dependencies**: Requires hangrier_games-ae4 (event integration) to be completed first

Currently, refresh tokens are revoked during token rotation when using the /refresh endpoint, but there's no explicit logout endpoint.

- Revokes the token in the database
- Returns 204 No Content on success
- Returns appropriate error if token doesn't exist

This gives users a way to explicitly invalidate their session.

title: Tribute sponsorship system
description:
Players can act as sponsors, spending currency to send gifts to specific tributes during games.

**Vision**: Classic Hunger Games sponsor mechanic - wealthy viewers help their favorite tributes with supplies.

**Core Mechanics**:
- Sponsor Coins (SCC): Currency earned by watching games, login bonuses
- Gift catalog with various items at different price points
- Sponsor selects tribute (by name or district), chooses gift, pays SCC
- Gifts delivered at start of tribute's next turn

**Gift Catalog**:
| Item     | Cost | Effect                     |
| -------- | ---- | -------------------------- |
| Bread    | 5    | +5 health                  |
| Medicine | 15   | Cure status effect         |
| Weapon   | 30   | Give tribute a weapon      |
| Shield   | 25   | +3 defense for 1 turn      |
| Map      | 10   | Reveal all tributes 1 turn |
| Signal   | 20   | Reveal items in area       |

**Delivery**:
- Items appear in tribute's inventory
- "Package falls from sky!" announcement
- Timing matters: gift before big fight

**Anti-griefing**:
- Cannot sponsor enemies (or can sponsor ironically)
- Gift recipient visible to all (or anonymous?)

**Dependencies**: Real-time updates (36t) recommended but not required

**Leaderboard**: Sponsor leaderboard (most gifts sent)

title: Live player-controlled tribute turns
description:
Convert game from autonomous AI simulation to player-controlled turns.

**Vision**: When it's a player's tribute's turn, they get to pick from a list of actions. Creates real-time drama and player engagement.

**Core Mechanics**:
- Game modes: AI simulation vs Live (player-controlled) vs Hybrid
- Turn system: Queue rotation through tributes, current_turn_tribute_id
- Turn timeout (e.g., 30 seconds), auto-skip to AI after timeout
- Action selection via API: POST /api/games/{id}/tributes/{id}/action

**Action Types**:
- Attack (target tribute)
- Defend (boost defense)
- Move (to adjacent area)
- Hide (avoid detection)
- Search (find items)
- Use Item (consume from inventory)
- Wait (skip turn)

**Frontend**:
- "Your turn!" notification
- Action picker UI with valid targets highlighted
- Countdown timer
- Hybrid mode: show which tributes are player-controlled vs AI

**Dependencies**: Real-time updates (36t) recommended for live gameplay

**Complexity**: High - requires rethinking entire game loop

title: JWT refresh token rotation

**Implementation**:

- Return new access token on valid refresh token
- Revoke old refresh token on logout

title: Add per-area enemy-density signal to choose_destination scoring
description:
Long-term: when all areas share terrain (default Clearing on hex map), choose_destination returns the first tied area which on the smart-path = tribute's own area, leading every crowded tribute to Rest. PR #183 patches this at the call site by escaping to cheapest neighbor when crowded.

Better fix: pass per-area enemy counts (or living tribute density) into choose_destination and apply a "crowd penalty" so a non-current empty area always outscores the current crowded one. Eliminates the call-site special case and naturally produces dispersion behavior.

Files: game/src/tributes/brains.rs (choose_destination signature + scoring), game/src/games.rs (collect tributes_by_area density and pass through).

title: Replace AreaEvent fallback for cycle announcements with typed CycleAnnouncement payload
description:
Game::log() in game/src/games.rs synthesises a MessagePayload::AreaEvent with area.name = game uuid + empty description for MessageSource::Game cycle announcements (announce_cycle_start/end). Frontend works around it (PR #182) but the right fix is a dedicated CycleAnnouncement variant in shared::messages::MessagePayload, migrate announce_cycle_start/end + fallback_payload to use it, and route to a CycleAnnouncementCard (or include in StateCard) on the web side.

Affects: game/src/games.rs::fallback_payload (MessageSource::Game arm), announce_cycle_start, announce_cycle_end; shared/src/messages.rs::MessagePayload + MessageKind; web/src/components/timeline/event_card.rs routing; new CycleCard.

title: Empty games list still reproducible under aggressive click/refresh
description:
After PR #181 (auth_lock serializing JWT auth + handler), user can still occasionally land an empty /games list under repeated rapid refresh/navigation. Mutex closed the obvious race; residual cause unclear.

Hypotheses to investigate:
1. Frontend GamesListQ caches QueryError::NoGames on a transient 401 / network blip and shows NoGames before refetch resolves
2. tower_governor rate-limit (2 req/sec, burst 50) returns 429 → reqwest .send() Ok with non-2xx → games_list.rs:39 maps into `Err(QueryError::NoGames)` (no status check)
3. surreal_jwt returns 401 if `state.db.authenticate(jwt)` momentarily errors mid-flight → frontend treats as NoGames
4. Token refresh flow drops the bearer between mount and request

Next time it reproduces: capture network tab status code + body for /api/games, /tmp/api.log timestamps. If 429: improve UX (don't show NoGames on 429). If 401: investigate JWT lifetime / refresh wiring.

games_list.rs:30-46 currently lacks status discrimination — any non-Ok response.json() failure becomes BadJson, any reqwest send error becomes NoGames. That coalescing makes diagnosis harder.

- `WeatherChanged { area, from, to, phase }` — fires on Markov transition. Atmospheric narrative beats ("as night falls, a storm rolls into the Forest").

title: Brainstorm: Save and share replay state for progressive-display
description:
Persist and share replay state from the progressive-display Timeline.

Context: Out of scope from `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-progressive-display-design.md`. v1 resets replay state (cursor, paused/playing, speed) on each entry to a phase. This issue is about saving and sharing that state.

Two related-but-distinct features:

1. **Saving** — preserve replay state across navigation and browser sessions:
   - Cursor position per (game, phase) so returning to a phase resumes where the user left off
   - Speed multiplier preference saved globally per user (localStorage or user profile)
   - Auto-scroll-to-latest engaged/disengaged state probably ephemeral (don't save)

2. **Sharing** — generate URLs that deep-link to a specific replay position:
   - URL shape (e.g., `/games/:id/timeline?phase=N1&event=14&speed=2x&autoplay=true`)
   - Copy-link button in the transport bar
   - On load: hydrate Timeline state from URL params, optionally start playing
   - Useful for "watch this dramatic kill" sharing

Open questions:
- Where does saved state live? localStorage (per-browser) vs server-side per-user-profile (cross-device)
- Privacy: does sharing leak game state to people who shouldn't see it? Probably not — game data is already public via /games/:id, this just adds a starting cursor
- Should saved cursor expire (last seen > 30 days = reset)?

Implementation surface: web/ crate. Hooks into routes.rs for URL parsing; storage.rs for localStorage; transport bar UI.

Related: progressive-display spec.

title: Quickstart games should get a generated name (witty phrase) instead of "Unnamed Game"
description:
When the user clicks the **Quickstart** button (`web/src/components/create_game.rs::CreateGameButton`), the frontend sends `CreateGame { name: None, .. }`. The API (`api/src/games.rs::create_game`, line ~179) then falls back to the literal string `"Unnamed Game"`:

```rust
let game_name = payload.name.unwrap_or_else(|| "Unnamed Game".to_string());
```

The named-form path (`CreateGameForm`) sends a user-supplied name, so this branch is only reached for Quickstart. Result: the games list shows multiple identical "Unnamed Game" rows, which is bad UX.

`Game::default()` already builds a witty name via `WPGen::with_words(3)` (see `game/src/games.rs::Default`), so the cleanest fix is to either:

1. Have the API call `Game::default().name` when `payload.name` is `None`, or
2. Have the Quickstart frontend generate the name itself before sending the DTO (less ideal — duplicates the WP generator).

Acceptance:
- Clicking Quickstart produces a game whose `name` is a hyphenated 3-word phrase from `WPGen`.
- The named form path still uses the user-supplied name unchanged.
- `cargo check -p api` and `cargo test -p game` pass.
- Manual: clicking Quickstart twice produces two distinct random names in the games list.
close_reason: Filed in error — that is a follow-up nit, not the bug the user is reporting (Quickstart not creating a new game).

title: Port api crate to surrealdb 3.x types and query API (sql::Thing/Datetime, bind, RecordId)
description:
When SurrealDB 3.x upgrade unblocks, port API code from `surrealdb 2.x` `sql::*` types to 3.x equivalents. Known surface (from `grep '^use surrealdb' api/src api/tests`):

- `api/src/auth.rs`: `use surrealdb::sql::{Datetime, Thing}` → likely `surrealdb::RecordId` + `chrono::DateTime<Utc>`.
- `api/src/users.rs`: `use surrealdb::sql::Thing`, `surrealdb::sql::thing(&claims.id)`, `surrealdb::opt::auth::Record`. `thing()` parser likely moved/renamed.
- `api/src/games.rs`, `api/src/tributes.rs`: `surrealdb::sql::Thing`, `surrealdb::RecordId`. Verify `db.query(...).bind((k, v))` signature — 3.x may want `bind(("k", v))` vs `bind(k, v)`.
- `api/tests/common/mod.rs`: `surrealdb::engine::any::connect(("mem://", config))` — verify in-mem connect signature.
- `schemas/*.surql`: `DEFINE FUNCTION fn::*` syntax may need updates (return type annotations, `<future>` etc.).
- `migrations/definitions/_initial.json`: schema-format version may need regeneration if format changed.

This is the porting follow-up to hangrier_games-7kd, separated so the migration-tooling decision (hangrier_games-qt3) can be tackled independently.

title: Decide migration strategy for SurrealDB 3.x (subprocess vs hand-rolled vs pin client)
description:
Decide and document an approach to keep startup migrations working when we move to surrealdb 3.x. Options:

(A) Shell out to the `surrealkit` CLI binary at API startup. Requires bundling the binary in api.Dockerfile and devcontainer Dockerfile (currently pinned to surreal v2.1.4). Awkward for in-process `kv-mem` integration tests.
(B) Hand-roll a minimal in-process migration runner: read `schemas/*.surql` (and maybe `migrations/*.surql`) in a deterministic order and execute via the SDK. Loses the version tracking / definitions snapshotting `surrealdb-migrations` provides. Need to re-implement "applied migrations" tracking (probably a `migrations` table).
(C) Pin surrealdb client to 2.x even after server moves to 3.x (only viable while the 2.x client can still talk to a 3.x server — verify).

Output: a short ADR in docs/ recording the choice, plus a beads task to implement it.

title: Watch surrealkit for embeddable library API (unblocks SurrealDB 3.x upgrade)
description:
When `surrealkit` ships an embeddable library API (or upstream releases an alternative migration crate that supports surrealdb 3.x), unblock and re-execute the surrealdb 3.x upgrade.

Watch points:

title: Upgrade SurrealDB 2.x → 3.x (blocked on migration tooling)
description: surrealdb 3.0.5 is the current major release. Touches api/Cargo.toml direct dep + all platform-specific overrides + dev-dependencies. surrealdb-migrations also has a corresponding 3.x line (currently we're on 2.4.0 which still works against 2.x). Risks: query syntax changes, schema migration tool compatibility, migrations/definitions/_initial.json may need regeneration, custom functions (fn::get_full_game etc) may need rewriting. Plan: (1) read upstream 2.x→3.x migration guide, (2) bump surrealdb in api/Cargo.toml, (3) verify migrations apply cleanly to a fresh .surrealdb/, (4) run api integration tests, (5) re-test schema queries via Surrealist. Do not attempt before all other major upgrades land.
notes:
Attempted 2026-05-01. Blocked by ecosystem state:

1. `surrealdb-migrations` (currently used at startup via `MigrationRunner::new(&db).up()`) was archived April 2026 and only supports surrealdb 2.x. No 3.x release exists.
2. Upstream replacement is `surrealkit`, but it is a CLI binary, not a library — `surrealkit-0.5.8 is not a library` per docs.rs. Cannot be embedded in `api/src/main.rs` startup the same way.
3. surrealdb 3.x also reorganized `surrealdb::sql::{Thing, Datetime, thing}` (likely `RecordId` + `chrono::DateTime`), and may change `bind`/`DEFINE FUNCTION` semantics. Real porting work in `api/src/{auth,users,games,tributes}.rs`, `api/tests/common/mod.rs`, and `schemas/*.surql`.

Decision: stay on surrealdb 2.x for now. Reopen when:
- surrealkit ships a usable embeddable library API, OR
- we accept a startup-time subprocess shellout to `surrealkit sync`, OR
- we hand-roll a minimal in-process `.surql` migration runner.

See follow-ups: hangrier_games-* (filed alongside this).

title: Optimize user ID extraction by decoding JWT
description:
Currently in user_create and user_authenticate endpoints, after getting the JWT from SurrealDB's signup/signin, we query the database again to get the user's ID:

```rust
let jwt = auth_result.into_insecure_token();
// Then: SELECT id FROM user WHERE username = $username
```

This is inefficient. Instead, decode the JWT to extract the user ID directly from the 'id' claim. This:
- Eliminates unnecessary database query
- Reduces latency
- Simplifies the code

The JWT already contains the user ID in the 'id' field of the claims (we set it in generate_access_token). Use jsonwebtoken crate's decode function with validation disabled (we trust our own tokens).
close_reason: Completed JWT optimization in hangrier_games-ecr worktree. Changes eliminate database query after authentication by decoding JWT claims directly. Code compiles successfully. Commit: 3973a4f2

title: Implement cleanup job for expired/revoked refresh tokens
description:
The refresh_token table will accumulate expired and revoked tokens over time, which can:
- Waste database storage
- Slow down queries  
- Create security concerns (more data to protect)

Implement a background cleanup job that:
- Runs periodically (e.g., daily at 3 AM)
- Deletes refresh tokens where (revoked = true OR expires_at < NOW())
- Logs the number of tokens cleaned up
- Can be triggered manually via admin endpoint

Consider using a task scheduler or cron-style library for Rust (e.g., tokio-cron-scheduler).
close_reason: Implemented cleanup job for expired/revoked refresh tokens. Background job runs daily at 3 AM UTC using tokio-cron-scheduler. Deletes tokens where revoked=true or expires_at < NOW(). Logs number of tokens cleaned up.

**Fun element**: Emotional moment - you see your dead teammate, rush to meet them, only to realize it's a ghost.

title: Reconcile MessageKind with GameEvent (mqi child 6/6)
description:
Once GameEvent is persisted on every message, MessageKind enum becomes redundant — it duplicates discriminant info already encoded in GameEvent.

Tasks:
- Decide: derive MessageKind from GameEvent at read time, or drop MessageKind column + index entirely.
- If dropping: schema migration to remove kind column, update API filter helpers, frontend kind matches become event matches.
- If keeping (as a fast index): document why and add From<&GameEvent> for MessageKind so the two cannot drift.

Acceptance:
- No drift possible between MessageKind and GameEvent variant
- API filter performance characteristics documented either way
- All gates green

Depends on mqi.5. P4 because it's cleanup, not blocking value.
acceptance_criteria: MessageKind either dropped or pinned-derived from GameEvent; no drift possible.

value: Tribute AI: Brain component uses strategy pattern based on enemy count (0, 1-5, 6+). Decision thresholds: health (1-19, 20-40, 41+), sanity (10, 20, 35), movement (10), intelligence (35, 80). Outputs Action enum: Rest/Hide/Move/Attack/UseItem/None. Located in game/src/tributes/brains.rs

Tech stack: Axum 0.8.4, SurrealDB 2.3.2, Tokio 1.45.0, Dioxus 0.6.3, dioxus-query 0.7.0 (React Query pattern), Tailwind CSS, gloo-storage, rand/rstest, ollama-rs with custom announcers model (qwen2.5:1.5b).

Dev commands: just dev (full stack), just api, just web, just test (game only), just fmt (edition=2024), just quality (all checks), just build-css (Tailwind). Dioxus: dx serve. Custom rustfmt: fn_single_line=true.

Environment: .env required with APP_API_HOST, SURREAL_HOST, SURREAL_USER/PASS. Frontend reads APP_* at build time via build.rs → src/env.rs. WASM needs RUSTFLAGS='--cfg getrandom_backend="wasm_js"' and wasm32-unknown-unknown target.

Known limitations: No JWT refresh tokens. No rate limiting. CORS allows any origin in dev. No input validation (relies on Serde). Some endpoints use .expect() instead of error handling. No pagination on game/tribute lists. logging.rs custom tracing unused. (Updated 2026-04-19: GLOBAL_MESSAGES and save_global_messages_to_db placeholder no longer exist — event unification per 33r landed Game.messages buffer + drain in api/src/games.rs.)

Game areas: 5-region arena (Cornucopia center + 4 cardinal directions). Areas have item inventories and can be dynamically closed. Tributes move between areas, items spawn/drop in areas. Area state tracked in SurrealDB with areas edge from game.

Auth: JWT with HS512, 1-hour expiry, no refresh tokens (known limitation). Argon2 password hashing via SurrealDB. Row-level permissions: public read/owner write for game/tribute, authenticated for area/item, owner-only for user.

Workspace: 5 crates - game/ (pure engine), api/ (Axum REST + SurrealDB), web/ (Dioxus WASM + Tailwind), shared/ (DTOs), announcers/ (Ollama LLM). Data flow: Frontend → API (JWT) → Game engine → SurrealDB → Announcers (optional).

Simulation: run_day_night_cycle() orchestrates turns. Each tribute acts via Brain::act(), emits messages. Combat uses d20 rolls. No direct tribute-to-tribute interaction - all state mutations through game. Items modify attributes, movement changes areas.

Testing: 60+ rstest unit tests in game crate. API integration tests in api/tests/: auth_tests (7/7), games_tests (10/10), game_customization_test (10/10), websocket_tests (4/4) all pass. simulation_tests (8) and tributes_tests (9) all ignored under hangrier_games-0jl: tests POST to /api/games/{id}/tributes which does not exist; tributes auto-spawn at game creation. Run integration tests with --test-threads=1 against running SurrealDB. Frontend: no tests yet.

Frontend state: dioxus-query for async query caching (React Query pattern). 6 context signals: loading, theme, game, delete_game, edit_game, edit_tribute. LocalStorage persistence via gloo-storage (JWT + theme). 3 themeable colorschemes. No state persistence for game data (always refetch from API).
Architecture: Pure functional core in game/ crate (no I/O, deterministic). API and web crates act as imperative shells. Event sourcing via GLOBAL_MESSAGES queue.

Database: SurrealDB graph with 8 tables. Graph edges: owns (tribute→item), playing_in (tribute→game), areas (game→area), items (area→item), summaries (game→summary). Custom functions: fn::get_full_game, fn::get_display_game, fn::get_full_tribute. Migrations auto-run at API startup.

Tribute attributes: health (0-100), sanity (0-100), movement (0-100), intelligence (0-100), bravery (affects decisions), is_hidden (visibility state). Status effects modify these values. Dead tributes (health=0) return Action::None. Brain preferred_action can override normal AI with percentage chance.

Items: Factory pattern with static methods (new_random_weapon, new_random_shield, new_random_consumable). Weapons/shields have attack/defense values. Consumables modify health/sanity/movement. Tributes can own multiple items (owns graph edge). Item locations tracked via items edge to areas.

logging.rs removed: Custom HangryGamesLogLayer was dead code in api/src/logging.rs. Commented out in main.rs initialize_logging() since it duplicated tracing_subscriber's built-in .json() layer. Removed entirely in commit e35c32f8. If structured JSON logging needed in future, use built-in fmt::layer().json() instead.
